title: Stack Widget in Flutter
content:
### Stack Widget

`Stack` places children on top of each other in a layered fashion. Children can be positioned using `Positioned` or `Align`.

```dart
Stack(
  children: [
    Container(
      width: 300,
      height: 300,
      color: Colors.blue,
    ),
    const Positioned(
      top: 20,
      left: 20,
      child: Text('Overlay'),
    ),
    const Positioned(
      bottom: 20,
      right: 20,
      child: Icon(Icons.star, color: Colors.white),
    ),
  ],
)
```

### Positioned Widget

`Positioned` controls the position of a child within a Stack.

```dart
Stack(
  children: [
    const FlutterLogo(size: 200),
    Positioned(
      left: 0,
      top: 0,
      right: 0,
      child: Container(
        color: Colors.black54,
        padding: const EdgeInsets.all(8),
        child: const Text(
          'Header',
          style: TextStyle(color: Colors.white),
          textAlign: TextAlign.center,
        ),
      ),
    ),
    Positioned(
      bottom: 0,
      right: 0,
      child: IconButton(
        icon: const Icon(Icons.favorite, color: Colors.red),
        onPressed: () {},
      ),
    ),
  ],
)
```

### Stack Properties

| Property | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `alignment` | `AlignmentGeometry` | Default alignment for non-positioned children |
| `fit` | `StackFit` | How non-positioned children size themselves |
| `clipBehavior` | `Clip` | Clipping behavior for overflow |

### StackFit Options

| Value | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `loose` | Child's original size (default) |
| `expand` | Stretch to stack size |
| `passthrough` | Pass constraints through |

### Common Patterns

```dart
// Badge on icon
Stack(
  children: [
    const Icon(Icons.notifications, size: 40),
    Positioned(
      right: 0,
      top: 0,
      child: Container(
        padding: const EdgeInsets.all(4),
        decoration: const BoxDecoration(
          color: Colors.red,
          shape: BoxShape.circle,
        ),
        child: const Text('3', style: TextStyle(color: Colors.white, fontSize: 10)),
      ),
    ),
  ],
)

// Gradient overlay on image
Stack(
  children: [
    Image.network('https://example.com/image.jpg'),
    Positioned.fill(
      child: Container(
        decoration: const BoxDecoration(
          gradient: LinearGradient(
            begin: Alignment.topCenter,
            end: Alignment.bottomCenter,
            colors: [Colors.transparent, Colors.black54],
          ),
        ),
      ),
    ),
  ],
)
```

> **Key Point:** Stack layers children from bottom to top. Use `Positioned` for exact placement. Use `Positioned.fill()` to cover the entire stack.

quiz.question: How does Stack arrange its children?
options: Layered on top of each other

quiz.question: Which widget positions a child within a Stack?
